name: ceratops_github_org_nondeterministic_contract
description: Organization checks whose outcome depends on intent, least privilege, security ownership, browser confirmation, or paid-feature classification.

notes: Browser confirmation is required only when API evidence is missing, ambiguous, plan-limited, or disputed.

review_result_values.pass: AI-agent review found the intent satisfied and recorded evidence.
review_result_values.fail: AI-agent review found a concrete mismatch.
review_result_values.approved_drift: The mismatch is allowed by the deterministic contract's approved drift profile.
review_result_values.blocked: Review needs unavailable permission, paid functionality, missing administration access, or browser UI access.
review_result_values.not_applicable: The organization plan, feature availability, or policy scope makes the check irrelevant.

checks.applies_when: Organization profile, logo, display name, billing email, blog, location, or public profile text is user-facing.
checks.review_required: Confirm the visible organization identity is intentional, current, and matches the intended source organization where byte equality is not the right rule.

checks.applies_when: The avatar hash cannot be fetched, GitHub returns transformed image variants, or browser parity is disputed.
checks.review_required: Confirm in the browser that the organization uses the intended custom logo rather than a generated default avatar or stale upload.

checks.applies_when: Members, owners, outside collaborators, custom roles, or security managers differ from contract expectations.
checks.review_required: Confirm the people and role set is intended, least-privilege, and not a stale invite, missing owner, or forgotten collaborator.
Confirm team names, descriptions, privacy, maintainers, inherited access, and repository access fit the organization operating model.

checks.applies_when: Organization webhooks, Actions secrets or variables, Dependabot secrets, private registries, or GitHub Apps affect behavior.
checks.review_required: Confirm each integration is still used, scoped to the correct repositories, and has a real owner; confirm hidden secret values with that owner when needed.

checks.applies_when: Actions policy, runner groups, selected actions, OIDC templates, retention, fork approval, or hosted compute settings differ or are unavailable.
checks.review_required: Confirm required workflows remain possible while unwanted action, runner, trigger, OIDC, retention, and fork exposure is prevented; classify preview and plan-limited controls.

checks.applies_when: Code-security configurations, defaults, security managers, dependency submission, or paid security surfaces are present or missing.
checks.review_required: Confirm the security posture is intentional, no-extra-cost features are enabled when available, and paid requirements are classified rather than silently required.

checks.applies_when: Dependabot repository access, secrets, or private registry configurations exist.
checks.review_required: Confirm access is limited to intended repositories and registries, credentials remain valid, and broad repository access is intentional.

checks.applies_when: Open Dependabot alerts or Dependabot-authored pull requests exist anywhere in the organization.
checks.review_required: Classify each item as active, fixed, superseded, blocked, intentionally retained, or safely mergeable; archived repository items are report-only unless explicitly reactivated.

checks.applies_when: Custom properties or a repository metadata schema exists.
checks.review_required: Confirm names, allowed values, required flags, defaults, and descriptions remain useful and do not encode stale taxonomy.

checks.applies_when: Blocked users, interaction limits, issue types or fields, banners, or reported-content policy differ or are unavailable.
checks.review_required: Confirm moderation state, intake fields, and announcement text are intentional and not stale incident response.

checks.applies_when: Organization rulesets or personal access token requests are enabled, unavailable, or plan-limited.
checks.review_required: Confirm rulesets and token approval policies match merge and credential posture; classify plan-limit errors and the intended repository-level fallback.

checks.applies_when: Any endpoint returns plan-limited, enterprise-only, or permission-limited state.
checks.review_required: Confirm whether the gap is paid functionality, missing permission, or true drift before deciding remediation.

checks.applies_when: GitHub renamed, moved, or plan-gated a setting, or API and browser state disagree.
checks.review_required: Recheck current official GitHub documentation and the browser UI before deciding drift.
